source_text: Allow 1-inch top, bottom, left, and right margins on each page. On letterhead paper, typing must start more than 1 inch from the top edge. Page numbers and footers on continuation pages must be positioned 0.5 inch from the bottom edge of the page. Do not right, center, or full justify text or use proportional spacing.
plain_language_rule: Standard letters must have 1-inch margins on all sides (top, bottom, left, right). On letterhead paper, the first line of typing must start more than 1 inch from the top edge. Page numbers and footers on continuation pages must be positioned 0.5 inch from the bottom of the page. Text must not be right-justified, center-justified, or fully justified. Proportional spacing must not be used.
renderer_impact: Page geometry must enforce 1-inch top/bottom/left/right margins. Top margin on page 1 must accommodate the letterhead (typing starts below the 1-inch threshold). Bottom margin must accommodate footers/page numbers at 0.5 inch from the bottom edge. Text must use left justification only; proportional spacing is not permitted.
notes: The manual specifies 1-inch margins on all four sides. On letterhead paper, typing starts more than 1 inch from the top. Page numbers and footers on continuation pages are 0.5 inch from the bottom. Text must be left-justified only; proportional spacing is prohibited. These are deterministic layout constraints specified directly by the manual.
